contradiction_text: The article presents AI agents as a productivity boost for experienced developers while also citing prior human-study results showing slowdown and very low merged-output success in other real-world settings.
related_claim_texts: Experienced developers in this study viewed AI agents as a productivity boost rather than as autonomous substitutes for their own work.
Positive developer sentiment toward AI agents in this study was conditional on the developers' ability to compensate for agent limitations, not on trust in agent autonomy alone.
disclosure: Positive sentiment and reported usefulness should remain explicitly context-bound rather than read as a general performance verdict, because the paper itself cites contrary real-world results.

contradiction_text: The article describes agents as useful for coding work, but the same evidence narrows that usefulness to well-described, straightforward tasks and rejects suitability for complex tasks.
related_claim_texts: Developers in this study treated AI agents as suitable for well-described, straightforward tasks and unsuitable for complex tasks.
disclosure: Any statement of agent value must keep the task boundary visible; the evidence does not support a broad claim that agents are suitable across complex software work.

contradiction_text: The article treats agents as a productivity boost, but it also says developers had to plan in advance, supervise behavior, and validate all outputs, leaving unresolved how much of the gain survives the overhead of control.
related_claim_texts: Experienced developers in this study viewed AI agents as a productivity boost rather than as autonomous substitutes for their own work.
Experienced developers retained control over software design and implementation by planning work, supervising agent behavior, and validating outputs against core software quality standards.
disclosure: The article should keep visible that the reported benefit is not frictionless and that the provided material does not resolve the net cost of the required supervision and validation loop.
